title: Eduversal Principal Leadership Observation Guide
description: A structured framework for observing, documenting, and developing the leadership practice of school principals across the Eduversal Indonesia partner network. Used during scheduled school appraisal visits, termly leadership check-ins, and as the foundational reference for the Principal Weekly Checklist, the Annual Principal Appraisal, principal self-assessment, and other principal-facing modules. The guide is formative in nature: findings inform coaching and development conversations rather than producing a numerical score. Grounded in Indonesian regulation and aligned with Cambridge International school leadership expectations.

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendikbud Nomor 6 Tahun 2018 — Penugasan Guru sebagai Kepala Sekolah
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Defines the three core duties (tugas pokok) of the principal: managerial, entrepreneurial development, and supervision.

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendiknas Nomor 13 Tahun 2007 — Standar Kepala Sekolah/Madrasah
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Defines the five competency dimensions of the principal: personality, managerial, entrepreneurship, supervision, and social.

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendikdasmen Nomor 7 Tahun 2025 — Penugasan Guru sebagai Kepala Sekolah
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Current regulation governing principal appointment, term length (4 years × up to 4 periods), allowance, and rotation.

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendikdasmen Nomor 11 Tahun 2025 — Pemenuhan Beban Kerja Guru
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Defines the 37 jam 30 menit per week workload framework that the principal manages for staff.

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendikdasmen Nomor 13 Tahun 2025 — Pendekatan Pembelajaran Mendalam
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Current curriculum framework: deep learning approach, 8 dimensi profil lulusan, Coding & AI as elective subjects from Grades 5/7/10.

regulatory_alignment.title: PP 19/2005 jo. PP 13/2015 — Standar Nasional Pendidikan
regulatory_alignment.relevance: The eight national education standards (8 SNP) the principal is accountable for: isi, proses, SKL, PTK, sarpras, pengelolaan, pembiayaan, penilaian.

regulatory_alignment.title: PP Nomor 17 Tahun 2025 — Perlindungan Anak di Ranah Digital
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Digital child protection obligations relevant to safeguarding duties.

regulatory_alignment.title: Cambridge International Centre Approval Criteria
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Cambridge Centre requirements covering leadership, learning, safeguarding, and quality assurance.

indonesian_competency_mapping.kepribadian: Personal conduct and ethical leadership — see focus P8.
indonesian_competency_mapping.manajerial: Strategic planning and operations — see foci P1, P3.
indonesian_competency_mapping.kewirausahaan: Innovation, partnerships, sustainability — distributed across P1, P4, P6.
indonesian_competency_mapping.supervisi: Instructional leadership and staff supervision — see foci P2, P5.
indonesian_competency_mapping.sosial: Stakeholder and community engagement — see focus P6.

response_options.label: Established
response_options.description: Practice is consistently demonstrated, well-documented, and embedded in school routine. Evidence is readily available and corroborated by staff and records.

response_options.label: Developing
response_options.description: Practice is partially in place or inconsistently demonstrated. Some evidence exists but routines are not yet fully embedded; intent is clear but execution is uneven.

response_options.label: Not Observed
response_options.description: Not seen during this visit — may be due to visit timing or focus, or may indicate genuine absence of practice. A follow-up review is required to distinguish between the two.

foci.title: Strategic Vision & School Improvement Planning
foci.guiding_question: Does the principal articulate and operationalise a clear vision for the school's improvement journey?
foci.description: The observer notes whether the principal can clearly communicate the school's vision, mission, and strategic priorities, and whether these are translated into actionable planning documents (RKT, RKAS, school improvement plan). Evidence is sought of EDS-informed priorities, alignment with Eduversal and Cambridge expectations, and ongoing strategic dialogue with the yayasan, governors, and senior staff. Indicators include clarity of articulation, coherence between vision and resource allocation, and visible progress against stated priorities.
foci.prompt_for_observer: Describe how the principal articulated the school's vision and strategic priorities. Note the alignment between RKT/RKAS, EDS findings, and observable practice. Reference any evidence of strategic dialogue with yayasan, Eduversal, or staff.
evidence_indicators: Visi-misi visibly displayed and accurately recalled by staff and students
Current RKT and RKAS documents available and reflect EDS priorities
School improvement plan with measurable targets and review cadence
Minutes of strategic planning meetings (with yayasan, leadership team)
Alignment statement between school plan and Eduversal network priorities
Evidence of progress monitoring against the improvement plan
regulatory_anchor: Panduan Kerja Kepala Sekolah Bab II — Tugas Pokok dalam Pengembangan Sekolah
foci.theory_basis: Aligned with PSEL Standard 1 (Mission, Vision, and Core Values) and Robinson's leadership dimension of Establishing Goals and Expectations (effect size 0.42 on student outcomes).

foci.title: Instructional Leadership & Curriculum Quality
foci.guiding_question: Is the principal actively leading instructional quality and curriculum implementation?
foci.description: The observer notes the principal's direct involvement in instructional improvement: frequency and quality of classroom walkthroughs, engagement with curriculum design (national curriculum and Cambridge stages), monitoring of learning outcomes, and personal participation in learning conversations with teachers. Particular attention is given to the principal's literacy in current curriculum direction (Permendikdasmen 13/2025 deep learning, 8 dimensi profil lulusan, Coding & AI introduction) and Cambridge syllabus delivery.
foci.prompt_for_observer: Describe the principal's instructional leadership presence. Note frequency of walkthroughs, depth of curriculum knowledge, and the way the principal engages teachers in conversations about teaching and learning.
evidence_indicators: Walkthrough records show regular principal participation (target: ≥2 per week)
Principal can name current Cambridge curriculum focus and challenges by subject
Principal articulates the school's approach to Permendikdasmen 13/2025 deep learning
Curriculum maps and schemes of work are quality-assured by the principal
Evidence of principal-led instructional discussions in staff meetings
Learning outcome data is reviewed termly with curriculum leaders
regulatory_anchor: 8 SNP — Standar Isi, Standar Proses, Standar Kompetensi Lulusan
foci.theory_basis: Aligned with PSEL Standard 4 (Curriculum, Instruction, and Assessment) and Robinson's leadership dimension of Promoting and Participating in Teacher Learning and Development (effect size 0.84 — highest of five leadership dimensions).

foci.title: Managerial & Operational Effectiveness
foci.guiding_question: Are day-to-day school operations, resources, and routines well-managed?
foci.description: The observer notes the quality of school operations under the principal's stewardship: timetabling and staffing, RKAS execution and financial discipline, facilities (sarpras) condition and management, calendar coordination, and communication routines. Indicators include the orderliness of operational documentation, clarity of internal communication channels, and the principal's command of operational data.
foci.prompt_for_observer: Describe the operational state of the school. Note the quality of timetables, financial documentation, facilities, and routine communication. Reference the principal's responsiveness to operational issues raised during the visit.
evidence_indicators: Master timetable and staffing roster current and aligned with Permendikdasmen 11/2025 (37 jam 30 menit)
RKAS execution reports up to date with variance analysis
Facilities log (sarpras) showing maintenance and procurement records
School calendar published and current; routines for parent and staff communication documented
Principal can answer operational questions (enrolment, staff count, key dates) without consulting records
Crisis and contingency procedures documented and rehearsed
regulatory_anchor: 8 SNP — Standar Pengelolaan, Standar Sarpras, Standar Pembiayaan
foci.theory_basis: Aligned with PSEL Standard 9 (Operations and Management) and Leithwood's category of Improving the Instructional Programme (organisational management as enabler of instruction).

foci.title: Quality Assurance & Data-Driven Improvement
foci.guiding_question: Is the principal using data systematically to drive school improvement?
foci.description: The observer examines the principal's use of multiple data sources — Rapor Pendidikan, EASE assessment results, Cambridge examination outcomes, internal formative data, EDS findings, satisfaction surveys, and dashboards — to identify priorities, design interventions, and evaluate impact. Indicators include the principal's ability to read and discuss data confidently, the visible link between data findings and resource decisions, and the cadence of data review.
foci.prompt_for_observer: Describe how the principal uses data. Note which sources are consulted, the depth of analysis, and the visible link between data and decisions. Reference any dashboards, reports, or reviews available during the visit.
evidence_indicators: Rapor Pendidikan results discussed in leadership meetings with action items
EASE assessment report acted upon, with intervention plan
Cambridge results analysed by subject, with strengths and areas for development identified
Stakeholder satisfaction surveys (staff, parent, student) reviewed termly
Principal can interpret a data point in context (not just recite numbers)
Visible link between data findings and RKAS spending or PD priorities
Use of Eduversal dashboards (EASE, Appraisals, Cambridge Performance) is evident
foci.theory_basis: Aligned with Hamilton, Halverson et al. on Data-Informed Decision Making and Bryk & Schneider's framework on Trust and Improvement Cycles in schools.

foci.title: Staff Supervision, Development & Wellbeing
foci.guiding_question: Is the principal building staff capacity, managing performance, and protecting staff wellbeing?
foci.description: The observer reviews the systems and practices through which the principal develops and supervises pendidik dan tenaga kependidikan (PTK): teacher appraisal (TAS) implementation, PKG (penilaian kinerja guru), recruitment and induction, mentoring and coaching arrangements, professional development planning, performance conversations, and attention to staff wellbeing. Particular attention is paid to whether supervision is developmental (not punitive) and whether PD is needs-based.
foci.prompt_for_observer: Describe the principal's approach to staff supervision and development. Note evidence of TAS or PKG implementation, PD planning, mentoring routines, and any wellbeing initiatives. Reference how performance conversations are structured.
evidence_indicators: Teacher Appraisal System (TAS) implementation evidence — observation cycles, appraisal records
PKG / SKP records current and signed
Annual PD plan tied to appraisal findings and school priorities
Induction programme for new teachers documented
Mentoring or coaching pairs identified and meeting regularly
Staff wellbeing indicators tracked (retention, absence, satisfaction)
Use of PMM / Pengelolaan Kinerja platform is current and accurate
regulatory_anchor: 8 SNP — Standar Pendidik dan Tenaga Kependidikan
foci.theory_basis: Aligned with PSEL Standard 6 (Professional Capacity of School Personnel) and Hallinger's framework on Leadership for Learning (teacher professional learning as central lever).

foci.title: Stakeholder & Community Engagement
foci.guiding_question: Is the principal building productive relationships with parents, yayasan, dinas pendidikan, and the wider community?
foci.description: The observer examines the principal's engagement with the full stakeholder ecosystem: parents and komite sekolah, yayasan and governors, dinas pendidikan and other regulatory bodies, alumni, partner schools, and the local community. Indicators include the cadence and quality of communication, the principal's visibility, the presence of structured engagement routines, and evidence that stakeholder voice influences school decisions.
foci.prompt_for_observer: Describe the principal's stakeholder engagement. Note routines for parent communication, komite sekolah meetings, yayasan reporting, and any community partnerships. Reference how stakeholder feedback is captured and acted upon.
evidence_indicators: Parent communication calendar and channels documented and active
Komite sekolah meets regularly with minutes available
Yayasan reporting cycle defined and current
Records of dinas pendidikan correspondence and visits
Alumni engagement initiatives (where relevant)
Stakeholder feedback mechanisms (surveys, suggestion channels) in use
Principal personally visible at key parent and community events
foci.theory_basis: Aligned with PSEL Standard 8 (Meaningful Engagement of Families and Community) and Epstein's Six Types of Parental Involvement framework.

foci.title: Compliance, Safeguarding & Governance
foci.guiding_question: Is the school operating in full compliance with Indonesian regulation, Cambridge requirements, and safeguarding standards?
foci.description: The observer reviews the school's compliance posture: Indonesian regulatory compliance (perizinan, accreditation, dapodik, kurikulum), Cambridge Centre approval requirements, child safeguarding policy and practice (including PP 17/2025 digital child protection), data protection, health and safety, and the school's governance structure. Indicators include the currency of compliance documentation, the embeddedness of safeguarding routines, and the principal's personal accountability for compliance posture.
foci.prompt_for_observer: Describe the school's compliance posture across regulatory, Cambridge, and safeguarding domains. Note the currency of key documentation and the principal's personal command of compliance status. Reference safeguarding routines observed during the visit.
evidence_indicators: Operational license (izin operasional) current and displayed
Accreditation status (BAN-SM) current; readiness file maintained
Cambridge Centre approval status and requirements file maintained
Dapodik current and accurate
Safeguarding policy current; designated safeguarding lead identified
Child protection routines visible (visitor logs, identification, reporting channels)
PP 17/2025 digital child protection measures in place
Data protection practices for student records observed
Health, safety, and emergency procedures documented and rehearsed
foci.theory_basis: Aligned with Cambridge International Centre Approval framework, UNICEF Child Safeguarding Standards, and PSEL Standard 9 (Operations and Management — compliance dimension).

foci.title: Personal Conduct & Professional Presence
foci.guiding_question: Does the principal model the professional conduct, ethical leadership, and growth mindset expected of an Eduversal school leader?
foci.description: The observer notes the principal's personal conduct during the visit and as reported by staff: punctuality, communication style, transparency in decision-making, ethical conduct, modelling of the values the school espouses, openness to feedback, and visible commitment to personal growth. This focus area maps directly to the kompetensi kepribadian dimension of Permendiknas 13/2007 and is foundational to all other foci.
foci.prompt_for_observer: Describe the principal's professional presence during the visit. Note how the principal communicates, makes decisions, handles disagreement, and models the school's values. Reference any observable indicators of growth mindset and openness to feedback.
evidence_indicators: Punctuality and preparedness for the visit
Communication style is clear, respectful, and professional with all stakeholders
Decisions are explained with reasoning; transparency in trade-offs
Principal models the values the school espouses (e.g. learning, integrity, respect)
Openness to feedback observed during the visit conversation
Evidence of own professional learning (PD log, reflective practice)
Conduct toward students is warm, professional, and developmentally appropriate
foci.theory_basis: Aligned with PSEL Standard 2 (Ethics and Professional Norms) and Fullan's writing on the Moral Imperative of School Leadership.

narrative_fields.label: Headline Strength
narrative_fields.description: The single most notable leadership strength observed during this visit. Specific, evidence-anchored, and worth recognising in conversation.

narrative_fields.label: Priority Development Area
narrative_fields.description: The single most important area for the principal's leadership development before the next visit. Specific, actionable, and framed as a development opportunity rather than a deficit.

narrative_fields.label: Agreed Follow-up Actions
narrative_fields.description: Specific actions agreed during the post-visit conversation, with named owners and target dates. Includes any commitments from Eduversal as well as from the principal.

narrative_fields.label: Principal's Reflection
narrative_fields.description: The principal's own reflection on the visit and the agreed development areas, captured in their own words during the conversation.

narrative_fields.label: Confidential Note to Eduversal
narrative_fields.description: An optional confidential note for Eduversal central records, not shared with the principal. Use sparingly and only for matters of safeguarding, governance concern, or escalation.

derivative_modules.description: This rubric is the foundational reference for the following principal-facing modules. Each module inherits the focus structure (P1–P8), regulatory anchors, and evidence indicators defined here.

modules.title: Principal Weekly Checklist
modules.purpose: A self-managed weekly routine for principals, mapping each focus area to specific weekly cadences (e.g. P2 → 2 walkthroughs/week; P4 → Friday data review).

modules.title: Principal Annual Appraisal
modules.purpose: End-of-year evaluative appraisal, scored, drawing on multiple visits and 360 feedback.

modules.title: Principal Self-Assessment
modules.purpose: Termly self-rating against the eight foci, with reflective narrative.

modules.title: Principal 360° Feedback
modules.purpose: Structured feedback from staff, parents, and students against the eight foci.

modules.title: Principal Coaching Conversation Template
modules.purpose: Structured conversation guide for Academic Director coaching sessions, anchored to the eight foci.

modules.title: Principal Induction Handbook
modules.purpose: Onboarding framework for newly appointed principals, structured around the eight foci.
